mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a statistically significant positive return differential over 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases yield negative 5-day forward returns in > 50% of observed cases during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently coincide with positive 20-day equity returns without subsequent rate hike expectations materializing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of payroll growth and unemployment decline fails to predict positive returns with an information coefficient above 0.04 over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth and higher corporate revenues, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals supply chain disruptions and weakening manufacturing demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions greater than 1% are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of historical instances.
